symbols.name: extractTimestampFromFilename

Extract timestamp from session filename.
Supports canonical Agent Vault session names like YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS-slug.md.

symbols.name: parseSessionNote

symbols.name: determineContinuationTarget

symbols.name: askUserConfirmation

symbols.name: loadContinuationContext

symbols.name: createContinuationSession
symbols.doc: Step 4: Create a continuation session

symbols.name: runReadinessCheck

symbols.name: queryCodeGraphIndex
symbols.signature: (index: CodeGraphIndex, { query, limit, pathSubstring, exportedOnly }: QueryCodeGraphIndexInput): CodeGraphLookupMatch[]

symbols.name: formatCodeGraphLookupResultsAsToon
symbols.signature: (index: CodeGraphIndex, matches: CodeGraphLookupMatch[], query: string, options: FormatCodeGraphLookupToonOptions = ...): string

symbols.name: STEP_MIRROR_CONTEXT_ID_KEY
symbols.doc:
Step-mirror frontmatter keys.

These fields are copied FROM the canonical session context ONTO step notes
to provide durable routing and continuity across sessions. They must NEVER
diverge from the canonical session — always read the source of truth from
the session note and mirror downstream.

Mirrors update only on:
- Session creation linked to the step
- Session lifecycle transitions (active → paused, paused → active, etc.)
- Session completion
- A new session becoming the active session for the step

Mirrors are intentionally minimal to avoid making the step note a second
source of truth (see DEC-0001).

symbols.name: buildStepMirror
symbols.doc: Build the step-mirror frontmatter object from canonical session state.

symbols.name: VaultNoteCandidate
symbols.doc:
A vault-note candidate for deterministic context ranking.

This type mirrors the shape of `VaultGraphNode` but only carries the fields
that the ranking function actually needs.  It is produced by the caller
(e.g. the context compiler) and passed into `rankContextCandidates`.

symbols.name: SourceFileCandidate
symbols.doc: A source-file candidate for deterministic context ranking.

symbols.name: ContextRankingOptions
symbols.doc:
Optional reference time for deterministic recency scoring.
If not provided, `Date.now()` is used.

symbols.name: ContextRankingInput
symbols.doc:
Input to the deterministic context ranker.

All task signals are optional; the ranker works purely on structural
signals if no task text is supplied.

symbols.name: RankedItem
symbols.doc: A single ranked item produced by the context ranker.

symbols.name: ContextRankingResult
symbols.doc: Result of a ranking pass.

symbols.name: rankContextCandidates
symbols.doc:
Rank vault-note and source-file candidates deterministically against task
signals, returning scored, ordered results with explainable reasons.

Same inputs always produce the same scores and the same ordering.
No embeddings, GPUs, network calls, or model availability requirements.

symbols.name: readNoteResource
symbols.signature: async (vaultRoot: string, uri: URL | string): Promise<ReadResourceResult>

symbols.name: readCodeStubResource
symbols.signature: async (vaultRoot: string, projectRoot: string, uri: URL | string): Promise<ReadResourceResult>

symbols.name: readCodeSummaryResource
symbols.signature: async (vaultRoot: string, projectRoot: string, uri: URL | string): Promise<ReadResourceResult>

symbols.name: readCodeExcerptResource
symbols.signature: async (vaultRoot: string, projectRoot: string, uri: URL | string): Promise<ReadResourceResult>

symbols.name: readContextResource
symbols.signature: async (vaultRoot: string, projectRoot: string, uri: URL | string): Promise<ReadResourceResult>

symbols.name: DEFAULT_IO
symbols.doc:
`vault migrate` command handler (implementation checklist PR-4 and PR-5).

The default (and `--dry-run`) path is strictly read-only plan mode.
`--apply` executes pending registry steps in order via `applyMigrations`,
running the full validate-all suite after each step's writes and refreshing
the code graph after any run that advanced the schema version.
`--apply --to <version>` stops at the named intermediate version.

symbols.name: MigrationPlanStepError
symbols.doc: Wraps a detect()/plan() failure so the failing step id is always reported.

symbols.name: withStepErrorContext
symbols.doc:
Re-throws detect()/plan() errors annotated with the owning step id so plan
failures never surface as anonymous errors (or worse, partial success).

symbols.name: createPostStepValidator
symbols.doc:
Default post-step validator: runs the full validate-all suite against the
vault after each step's writes. Validator errors fail the migration for
that specific step; warnings are surfaced but never treated as failures.

symbols.name: handleMigrateCommand
symbols.signature: async (argv: string[], environment: AgentVaultCommandEnvironment = ..., options: HandleMigrateCommandOptions = ...): Promise<number>

symbols.name: MIGRATION_REGISTRY
symbols.doc:
Ordered vault schema migration registry.

Registry shape (implementation checklist "Key Components"): one file per
migration step under `src/core/migrations/steps/`, imported here and listed
in ascending version order. The registry is append-only in practice — new
steps are added as new files; shipped steps are never edited in place.

Append-only: shipped steps stay in place and new steps are added after them.

symbols.name: latestSchemaVersion
symbols.doc:
The package's latest vault schema version, derived from the highest ordered
`to_version` in the registry. An empty registry means version `0`.

symbols.name: MigrationRegistryError
symbols.doc: Thrown when the registry itself is malformed. Always a maintainer error, never a vault-state error.

symbols.name: validateMigrationRegistry
symbols.doc:
Fail fast on malformed registries before any step runs.

Enforces, in order:
- unique step ids (no duplicates),
- each step upgrades forward (`to_version > from_version`),
- strictly ascending, contiguous ordering (each step's `from_version` equals
  the previous step's `to_version` — no gaps, no reordering, no duplicates),
- category/apply consistency (`unsafe-manual` steps MUST NOT define
  `apply()`; safe steps MUST define it).

symbols.name: MigrationPlanStatus
symbols.doc:
Migration runner (RFC "Safety Guarantees").

- `planMigrations` never writes to disk.
- Steps run strictly in ascending version order; a step is runnable only
  when its `from_version` matches the vault's current recorded version
  (no gap-jumping, no reordering).
- Unsafe/manual steps stop the run with a report; they are never skipped.
- The schema version advances only after a step's writes and the post-step
  validator succeed, so an interrupted apply resumes from the last completed
  step on re-run.

symbols.name: MigrationTargetError
symbols.doc: Thrown when a requested `--to` target version is invalid. Raised before any write.

symbols.name: planMigrations
symbols.doc: Plan the pending migrations for a vault. Never writes to disk.

symbols.name: assertValidTargetVersion
symbols.doc:
Validate a requested `--to` target version against the vault's current
version and the registry. Throws `MigrationTargetError` before any write.

symbols.name: applyMigrations
symbols.doc:
Apply pending migrations in strictly ascending order.

Stops and reports (never skips) at the first applicable unsafe/manual step.
The vault's `vault_schema_version` advances only after each step's writes
and the post-step validator succeed, keeping interrupted runs resumable.
When `targetVersion` is set, the run stops once that version is reached.

symbols.name: MigrationCategory
symbols.doc:
Risk classification fixed at authoring time.

- `safe-automatic`: purely additive or mechanically reversible; applied
  without extra confirmation.
- `safe-confirm`: content-changing but deterministic; plan output must show
  a per-note summary before apply.
- `unsafe-manual`: cannot be safely automated; MUST NOT define `apply()` and
  blocks automatic progression past its version.

symbols.name: MigrationStepContext
symbols.doc: Read-only context handed to every step hook. Steps must not touch paths outside `vaultRoot`.

symbols.name: MigrationStepPlan
symbols.doc: Structured, read-only description of what a step would change.

symbols.name: MigrationStep
symbols.doc:
One ordered vault schema migration.

`detect()` and `plan()` are always read-only. `apply()` performs the writes
and is required for safe categories; unsafe/manual steps omit it and instead
point maintainers at written upgrade guidance via their description.

symbols.name: updateStepMirrors
symbols.doc:
Re-read canonical session context from a session note and re-mirror onto the linked step.
This is the principled way to update step mirrors on lifecycle transitions, completion,
or when a new session becomes active for the step (per DEC-0001).
Returns true if the step note was modified, false otherwise.

symbols.name: MutationErrorCode
symbols.doc:
Conservative text-level mutations for Agent Vault notes.

These helpers intentionally touch only explicit frontmatter ranges,
named generated blocks, or leaf heading sections. If a note shape is
ambiguous, they fail instead of attempting a broad rewrite.

symbols.name: parseYamlFrontmatter
symbols.doc: Parse required YAML frontmatter and capture precise ranges for safe patching.

symbols.name: updateFrontmatter
symbols.doc:
Merge frontmatter updates while preserving unknown keys and note line endings.
Supports dot-path keys (e.g. `context.status`) for deep-merge into nested objects.
When a key contains dots, each segment is traversed/created as a nested object.
Non-dot keys and leaf segments use simple assignment (no deep-merge of objects).

symbols.name: replaceGeneratedBlock
symbols.doc: Replace only the contents of a named generated block.

symbols.name: readGeneratedBlockContent
symbols.doc: Read the contents of a named generated block without mutating the note.

symbols.name: readGeneratedBlockWithMarkers
symbols.doc: Read a named generated block including its start/end markers for bounded context excerpts.

symbols.name: readHeadingSectionContent
symbols.doc: Read a heading section, including the heading line and any nested subsections.

symbols.name: replaceHeadingSection
symbols.doc: Replace the body of a leaf heading section without disturbing neighboring sections.

symbols.name: appendToAppendOnlySection
symbols.doc: Append to a leaf heading section while preserving surrounding manual prose.

symbols.name: checkSchemaDrift
symbols.doc:
Compare the vault's recorded schema version (`.config.json`
`vault_schema_version`, absent config = version 0) against the package's
latest registry version.

Schema drift is always a warning, never an error (RFC "Validator
Relationship"): validation surfaces drift and points at `vault migrate`,
but existing pass/fail semantics stay stable. A behind vault warns toward
`vault migrate`; an ahead vault warns about the mismatch without suggesting
a downgrade. Reads only `.config.json` — no extra note scanning.

symbols.name: isProjectVault
symbols.doc:
Check whether a .agent-vault directory is a project vault (created by vault_init)
rather than a global runtime install directory.

symbols.name: prepareContext
symbols.doc:
Compile task-specific context by gathering, ranking, and rendering
candidates from vault notes, source files, git changes, and code graph.

Fails safely: if the code graph or stub cache is missing, degrades to
vault-note-only context and returns a hint to run vault_refresh.

symbols.name: parseFrontmatter
symbols.doc:
Parse YAML frontmatter from a markdown file.
Returns a simple key-value map (flat fields and arrays).

symbols.name: getBody
symbols.doc: Extract the body text after frontmatter.

symbols.name: extractTaskNames
symbols.doc: Extract task names from GSD plan XML-style task blocks.

symbols.name: extractObjective
symbols.doc: Extract the <objective> text from a plan.

symbols.name: scanPlanningPhases
symbols.doc: Scan .planning/phases/ for GSD phase directories.

symbols.name: extractSection
symbols.doc:
Read a section from a markdown file by heading name.
Returns the content under the heading until the next heading of equal or higher level.

symbols.name: backfillFromPlanning
symbols.doc:
Backfill vault from a .planning/ directory.

Called during initVault() after scaffold is created. Uses the existing
vault command handlers to create notes, ensuring all backreferences and
generated blocks are properly maintained.

symbols.name: enrichArchitectureStub
symbols.doc:
Enrich an architecture stub's generated block with imported data.
Only writes if the stub still has its placeholder content.

symbols.name: parseNamesFromBraces
symbols.doc:
Parse identifier names from brace content like "foo, bar as baz, qux".
For "as" aliases, uses the exported name (right side of "as").
Skips rest elements (...name) and nested destructuring.

symbols.name: extractJSDoc
symbols.doc:
Extract JSDoc comment from preceding lines.
Returns the comment text or undefined.

symbols.name: extractFunctionSignature
symbols.doc:
Try to extract a function signature from a single declaration line.
Returns undefined for multiline or complex declarations.

symbols.name: walkSourceFiles
symbols.signature: async (projectRoot: string, dir: string, results: FileSymbolsV3[], tieredAnalyzer?: TieredSourceAnalyzer, depth = ...): Promise<void>

symbols.name: writeCodeGraph
symbols.signature: async (projectRoot: string, vaultRoot: string, repoName: string): Promise<CodeGraphResult>

symbols.name: findSymbols
symbols.signature: (graph: CodeGraphResult, query: string): Array<{ file: string; symbol: CodeSymbolV3 }>

symbols.name: sanitizeStubPath
symbols.doc:
Convert a project-relative path like "src/core/vault-graph.ts" into a safe
stub filename like "src_core_vault-graph.ts.a1b2c3d4.stub.ts".

The hash prefix prevents collisions when multiple source paths map to the same
sanitized base name.

symbols.name: resolveStubPath
symbols.doc:
Resolve the vault-relative stub file path for a given source file.
Returns undefined if the stub doesn't exist on disk.

symbols.name: generateTypeScriptStub
symbols.doc:
Generate a TypeScript stub from source content.

Rules per DEC-0003:
- Do not invent behavior
- Keep exported declarations, public signatures, interfaces, types, enums, constants
- Keep useful docstrings where safely extractable
- Keep import/export relationships
- Drop function/method bodies
- Preserve line references when possible
- Mark incomplete/fallback stubs clearly

This is the initial regex-based parser; STEP-04-06 will add TypeScript compiler
and Tree-sitter backends for higher fidelity.

symbols.name: readStubManifest
symbols.doc: Read the stub manifest, returning undefined if it doesn't exist yet.

symbols.name: writeStubManifest
symbols.doc: Write the stub manifest atomically (write to temp, then rename — simplified: just overwrite).

symbols.name: isEntryStale
symbols.doc: Check if a manifest entry is still valid by comparing the stored hash with the current source file hash.

symbols.name: computeSourceMetadata
symbols.doc: Compute metadata for a source file: size, mtime, and SHA-256 hash.

symbols.name: generateStubForFile
symbols.signature: async (projectRoot: string, vaultRoot: string, projectRelativePath: string, parser: string = ...): Promise<GenerateStubResult | undefined>
symbols.doc:
Generate a stub for a single source file.
If a valid cached stub exists (same hash), returns without regenerating.

symbols.name: generateStubs
symbols.signature: async (projectRoot: string, vaultRoot: string, paths: string[], parser: string = ...): Promise<GenerateStubsResult>
symbols.doc:
Generate stubs for multiple source files at once, reusing the manifest.
Used by the context compiler when it needs stubs for indirect dependencies.

symbols.name: initializeStubCache
symbols.doc:
Initialize the stub cache directory and create an empty manifest.
Called during `vault_refresh target=code_stubs` or vault init.

symbols.name: invalidateStub
symbols.doc: Invalidate a specific stub entry from the manifest and remove its file.

symbols.name: AnalysisTier
symbols.doc:
Source Analyzer — parser abstraction with tiered fallback.

Tier order:
  1. TypeScript compiler (optional dynamic import from host environment)
  2. Tree-sitter (optional dependency, wasm-based web-tree-sitter)
  3. Regex (always available, wraps existing extractors from code-graph.ts)

Parser errors never break vault_refresh: failures downgrade per-file with
recorded warnings.

symbols.name: RegexAnalyzer
symbols.doc:
Wraps the existing regex-based extractors from code-graph.ts.
This is the always-available final fallback tier.

symbols.name: TypeScriptCompilerAnalyzer
symbols.doc:
Uses the TypeScript compiler API for high-fidelity extraction.
Loaded via optional dynamic import — `typescript` stays a devDependency
and is not promoted to a runtime dependency.

symbols.name: TreeSitterAnalyzer
symbols.doc:
Uses web-tree-sitter (wasm-based) for multi-language parsing.
Falls back gracefully when tree-sitter is not installed.

symbols.name: TieredSourceAnalyzer
symbols.doc:
Manages the tier chain: tries each analyzer in order, falling back on failure.
Records which tier produced each result and any warnings.

symbols.name: createTieredAnalyzer
symbols.doc: Create a fully-configured tiered analyzer with all three tiers.

symbols.name: AGENTS_MD_TEMPLATE
symbols.doc: Embedded AGENTS.md template, genericized with {{repo_name}} placeholder.

symbols.name: ARCH_SECTIONS
symbols.doc:
Empty architecture stub content for newly initialized vaults.
Each stub keeps the required headings but stays intentionally thin.

symbols.name: ACTIVE_CONTEXT_TEMPLATE
symbols.doc:
Embedded home note templates from 00_Home/.
Genericized with {{repo_name}}, {{repo_shape}}, and {{primary_stack}} placeholders.

symbols.name: PHASE_TEMPLATE
symbols.doc:
Embedded note templates from 07_Templates/.
These are the canonical template shapes for all vault note types.

symbols.name: OBSIDIAN_APP_CONFIG
symbols.doc:
Embedded Obsidian configuration for vault initialization.
workspace.json is intentionally excluded (too workspace-specific).

symbols.name: README_MD_TEMPLATE
symbols.doc: Embedded README.md template, genericized with {{repo_name}} placeholder.

symbols.name: AGENT_VAULT_MARKER
symbols.doc:
Template for the project-root AGENTS.md section that tells coding agents
about the Agent Vault and its MCP tools.

symbols.name: CODING_STANDARDS
symbols.doc:
Embedded shared knowledge templates from 06_Shared_Knowledge/.
Genericized: fred-specific coding standards (Effect idioms, runtime boundary rules)
have been removed. Generic standards about change hygiene and vault rules are kept.

symbols.name: REPO_VAULT_ROOT
symbols.doc:
Path to the canonical vault templates in this repo.
Tests copy templates from here to temp vaults so runtime generators stay aligned
with the checked-in vault contract.
